entities.name: Kubernetes
entities.how_described: 本集将其描述为一种难以管理的容器管理系统,主要为缓慢扩展的 Web 服务器设计,无法适应 AI 带来的突发性工作负载。Modal 构建更好运行时就是为了摆脱它的繁琐。

evidence.quote: It's because you have to run them on Kubernetes.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

evidence.quote: Kubernetes is hard to manage.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

evidence.quote: to burst up and down a lot versus like Kubernetes design for like slow scaling
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

evidence.quote: which is why would you have an agent read through hundreds of Kubernetes files
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

evidence.quote: So there is still a role for the always on large scale Kubernetes type of things.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_01

entities.name: 沙箱 (sandbox)
entities.how_described: 本集将沙箱描述为一种用于跑不受信代码、进行安全隔离的执行环境。Modal 在 2023 年 5 月就构建了沙箱,RL 爆发时有时瞬间需要十万个沙箱来跑策略部署。

evidence.quote: And right now it basically covers inference, training, batch crossing and sandbox workloads.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

evidence.quote: a gpu sandbox we can throw it on there they'll take a hugging face link
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_00

evidence.quote: let's say, you know, sandbox, persistent storage,
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

evidence.quote: A sandbox is actually a pod of containers, and you can run multiple containers in the sandbox.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

evidence.quote: people want a lot of control over outbound networking from a sandbox.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

evidence.quote: which is you can uh expose a tunnel inside a sandbox yeah and

entities.name: 推理 (inference)

entities.name: 投机解码 (speculative decoding)
entities.how_described: 本集解释了其原理:用一个小型草稿模型在前面预测 token,大模型随后批量验证。增加预测的接受长度能带来 2 到 4 倍的乘法级提速,且不会降低输出质量。

evidence.quote: how effective speculative decoding really just gets to anything you want
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_00

evidence.quote: to describe what speculative decoding is yes i think so we've covered like ego
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

evidence.quote: but it was like two years ago i think it doesn't hurt right the speculative decoding
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

entities.name: 智能体体验 (agent experience)
entities.how_described: 本集说 Modal 已把 SDK 团队的思考方式从「开发者体验(DX)」转变为「智能体体验(AX)」,即不让智能体读复杂的 YAML,而是在一个装饰器里做几处修改即可自我配置。

evidence.quote: We've actually changed our SDK team to think about agent experience sort
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

evidence.quote: and you've pivoted the team to agent experience is it like
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_01

evidence.quote: on just having better agent experience do you need something else
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_01

evidence.quote: for developer experience versus agent experience you seem to be on the side
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_01

entities.name: 快照 (snapshotting)
entities.how_described: 本集提到 Modal 整合了 GPU 快照功能,可以截取 GPU 的状态(如模型状态),让下一次调用启动得快得多,避免冷启动;也可用于智能体的状态持久化。

evidence.quote: snapshotting to the product so we can actually uh take
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

evidence.quote: because they were able to use some of the primitives like snapshotting
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

entities.name: 装饰器 (decorator)
entities.how_described: 本集描述其为一种代码语法,Modal 希望开发者或智能体不用花大量时间编写 YAML,而是直接在代码的装饰器里压缩并声明基础设施需求。

evidence.quote: in a decorator and it gets this sort of self-provisioning runtime of being able
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

evidence.quote: other feature which is you can add a decorator to a function and you get a cluster
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

entities.name: Auto Endpoints
entities.how_described: 本集说这是 Modal 推出的一种服务,让不想深度接触代码的用户也能从 UI 或 CLI 创建一个端点,内置了所有优化,但完全不设黑盒,用户随时可获取代码自行运行。

evidence.quote: auto endpoints is we want to make frontier level performance available to everyone
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

evidence.quote: to explain what auto endpoints are yeah i love you
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_00

entities.name: 容量池 (capacity pool)
entities.how_described: 本集提到 Modal 构建了一个跨越 17 个云提供商的容量池,使他们能比普通用户使用多得多、也便宜得多的云容量。

evidence.quote: which is we've built this capacity pool that spans 17 cloud providers.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

entities.name: RDMA
entities.how_described: 本集描述它是一种绕过 TCP 网络协议、在节点间极快传输数据的技术。在 Modal 上做多节点训练时,内部网络速度可达每秒 3 万亿比特。

evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_00

evidence.quote: The reason we have that is you need that to do the key exchange for RDMA

evidence.quote: before you set up the RDMA network on top of that.

evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_01

entities.name: Gitpod
entities.how_described: 本集提到它位于 CI/CD 市场(后改名 Ona),做的是构建时的沙箱;相比之下 Modal 做的是运行时沙箱。

evidence.quote: Gitpod, somewhat different market.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_01

evidence.quote: Yeah, people on our team have talked to the Gitpod team and they're technically very strong.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

evidence.quote: yeah so so uh for those who don't know gitpod rebranded

entities.name: Suno
entities.how_described: 本集提到这是一家做音频的公司,不在 Modal 上训练模型,但用 Modal 跑所有的推理,因为其模型架构完全自定义。

evidence.quote: So we kind of stayed away from the LM space and we were serving companies like Suno
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

evidence.quote: A better example is like let's say Suno because Suno does not use modal for training
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

entities.name: Runway
entities.how_described: 本集将 Runway 作为做视频的公司被提及,它使用 Modal 进行弹性推理部署。

evidence.quote: for audio, Runway for video.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

entities.name: Anthropic
entities.how_described: 本集在讨论基础模型实验室亲自下场做托管智能体时被提及,嘉宾认为这些大厂的托管产品适合作为起点。

evidence.quote: we're very excited to partner with Anthropic and some of the other foundation labs
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

entities.name: OpenAI
entities.how_described: 本集被提及为下场做托管智能体的 AI 巨头之一;同时提到 Gitpod 团队后来加入了 OpenAI。

evidence.quote: Brett Taylor, who's chairman of OpenAI, I was like, we need a new language for LLM.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_01

entities.name: Replicate
entities.how_described: 本集提到它是一个「倒下的竞争对手」,因为它只为模型提供 API,最终服务的是一个粘性极低的业余市场。

evidence.quote: This screen reminds me of a fallen competitor which Replicate.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_01

entities.name: Ramp
entities.how_described: 本集提到它作为构建生产级面向外部客户的智能体(如会计智能体)的案例,需要 Modal 提供的专业沙箱以获得更深的计算控制权。
